channel.title: EV on John's Music & Tech
channel.description: Recent content in EV on John's Music & Tech

item.title: Keeping the Bolt And Moving Up to the Equinox EV
item.description:
Editorial Note: I started writing the draft for this post from the passenger seat during our massive Memorial Day road trip down to Austin, Texas. It is now past the 4th of July, and I am just now getting around to polishing it up and clicking the publish button… sigh. Better late than never.

When I wrote my retrospective, Two Years With the Chevy Bolt: The Best Car I’ve Ever Owned, I praised how the electric drivetrain completely rewired my expectations of daily driving. I had taken a cautious first step with a 2023 model and walked away absolutely hooked. My only real regret back then? Wishing I had gone even bigger.

item.title: Two Years With the Chevy Bolt: The Best Car I’ve Ever Owned
item.description:
Two Years With the Chevy Bolt: The Best Car I’ve Ever Owned

I’ll say it upfront: the Chevy Bolt is the best car I’ve ever owned.

When I decided to go electric, I already knew the Bolt was nearing the end of its run. The 2023 model would be the last—and finding one wasn’t easy. Most dealers were out of stock, and the few that did get them sold them almost immediately.
